evidences.title: Court Dismisses Insurrection Warrants for Ex-Officials Park, Hwang
evidences.content: Published 2025.11.14. 01:38Updated 2025.11.14. 13:48 The bench warrant for former Minister of Justice Park Sung-jae, accused of participating in the December 3 emergency martial law despite knowing it was unconstitutional and illegal, was again rejected by the court on the 14th. Additionally, the bench warrant for former Prime Minister Hwang Kyo-ahn, who faced arrest over charges of inciting insurrection, was also dismissed by the court. Both Park and Hwang previously served as Minister of Justice. The consecutive rejection of bench warrants for the two individuals, requested by Cho Eun-suk’s insurrection special counsel team, is expected to act as a shockwave to the special counsel’s investigation. [...] EnglishNational # Court Dismisses Insurrection Warrants for Ex-Officials Park, Hwang Rejections hinder special counsel probe as court cites insufficient detention grounds By Kim Eun-gyeong, Oh Yu-jin Published 2025.11.14. 01:38Updated 2025.11.14. 13:48 [...] Park’s side, however, denied the allegations, stating, “We regret failing to prevent the emergency martial law, but the post-martial law document preparation was merely a directive to prepare for the Ministry of Justice’s d...

evidences.title: Court again denies arrest warrant for ex-justice minister
evidences.content: # Court again denies arrest warrant for ex-justice minister Former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae, center, arrives at the Seoul Central District Court in southern Seoul to attend his arrest warrant hearing, Thursday. Yonhap Former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae, center, arrives at the Seoul Central District Court in southern Seoul to attend his arrest warrant hearing, Thursday. Yonhap A court again denied a warrant Friday to arrest former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae on charges of involvement in former President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed imposition of martial law. [...] Park has been accused by special counsel Cho Eun-suk's team of playing a key role in an insurrection by calling a meeting of senior justice ministry officials following the martial law declaration Dec. 3, ordering the possible dispatch of prosecutors to a martial law-related organ, directing the Korea Correctional Service (KCS) to check the capacity of detention facilities and putting Korea Immigration Service officials in charge of travel bans on standby. Cho's team filed a second request for an arrest warrant earlier this week after the previous request was rejected by the Seoul Central District Court, which sta...

evidences.title: Special counsel again seeks arrest warrant for ex-justice minister in ...
evidences.content: # Special counsel again seeks arrest warrant for ex-justice minister in martial law probe Former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae appears for questioning at the special counsel’s office in Seoul, Oct. 24. Yonhap Former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae appears for questioning at the special counsel’s office in Seoul, Oct. 24. Yonhap A special counsel again applied for an arrest warrant for former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae on Tuesday over his alleged role in former President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed imposition of martial law. The application by special counsel Cho Eun-suk's team came after a court rejected its previous request to detain Park last month, citing insufficient evidence to justify detention. [...] Park is accused of playing a key role in Yoon's martial law imposition and alleged insurrection, such as by ordering the possible dispatch of prosecutors to a martial law-related organ, putting Korea Immigration Service officials in charge of travel bans on standby and directing the Korea Correctional Service (KCS) to assess detention centers' available capacity. Cho's team said it has re-summoned Park, re-seized his cell phone and confirmed additional criminal charges against...

Park is accused of aiding an insurrection and abusing his power during last year’s martial law crisis. (Choi Hyeon-su/Hankyoreh) A Seoul court on Wednesday rejected a request for an arrest warrant for former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae, who is suspected of playing a supporting role in former President Yoon Suk-yeol’s abortive declaration of martial law last year. [...] This is the second time a court has refused to grant an arrest warrant involving a Cabinet member under the Yoon administration, following the denial of a warrant for former Prime Minister Han Duck-soo, who is accused of aiding and abetting Yoon’s leadership of an insurrection. The special counsel team investigating the martial law crisis is expected to analyze the reasons for the court’s denial of the warrant before determining whether to seek another warrant. (2) The reason for the impeachment of the impeachment, which is set by Article 65 (1) of the Constitution, is a fact that a civil servant violates the Constitution or Law in its execution, and the law is not limited to criminal law. However, since the Constitution, as well as the provisions of laws that are not criminal law, do not have the same concreteness and clarity as the Criminal Law, the reason for impeachment cannot be required to be identified as the case of the criminal law. Therefore, it is sufficient to describe the facts in detail so that the resident can exercise the defense right and the constitutional court can confirm the judgment. However, in the case of the arrangement of detention facilities in the Seoul Eastern [...] emergency martial arts, it was hard to reinforce the president's resolution or facilitate the execution.
